response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. 

*** 

During the promotional week, the customer will pay less for both items. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will be discounted by 15%, which means the customer will pay 85% of the original price. The calculation for the table is $200 * 0.85 = $170. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will also be discounted by 15%, so the customer will pay 85% of $150, which is $127.50. 

Reach for the final amount, the customer will pay $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, totaling $297.50. The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers, which helps support local artisans and ensures that customers get high-quality products at a reduced price. 

*** 

The customer pays $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, totaling $297.50. The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers, which helps support local artisans and ensures that customers get high-quality products at a reduced price. The discount is applied separately to each item, ensuring that the customer pays the correct amount for each product.
